'''Gavin Clinch''' (born {{birth date|1974|09|13|df=y}}) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sh}} in the 1990s and 2000s.

Clinch played at representative level for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and at club level for [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], [[Penrith Panthers]], [[St. George Dragons]],<ref>Whiticker, Alan & Hudson, Glen (2006) The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players, Gavin Allen Publishing, Sydney</ref> [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (two spells), [[Wigan Warriors]], [[Huddersfield Giants#Summer era|Huddersfield]]-[[Sheffield Eagles#Merger|Sheffield]] Giants, and [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]].

Gavin Clinch moved to Halifax at the beginning of 1998's [[Super League III]] season and was that year selected in the [[Super League Dream Team]].<ref>[http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/local/GAVIN-CLINCHES-HAPPY-RETURN.314763.jp "Gavin Clinch's happy return"] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120509201529/http://www.halifaxcourier.co.uk/news/gavin-clinches-happy-return-1-1992019 |date=9 May 2012 }} 12 September 2000 ''[[The Halifax Evening Courier]]''</ref> He later won international caps for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] while at [[Huddersfield Giants#Summer era|Huddersfield]]-[[Sheffield Eagles#Merger|Sheffield]] Giants, and Halifax 2000–2001 1-cap plus 1 as substitute.<ref>{{cite book|first1= Graham|last1= Williams|first2= Peter|last2= Lush|first3= David|last3= Farrar|title= The British Rugby League Records Book|publisher= London League|isbn= 978-1-903659-49-6|year= 2009|pages= 104–105}}</ref>

In recent times, ‘The Pinch’, pioneered the Bosco Bulldogs Reserve Grade side to a Premiership in the 2023 Cronulla Sharks JRL Competition, finishing the year undefeated.
